title: Field-synchronous neutralization proxy specialist
summary: F1 strategy groups explicitly watch for Safety Car/VSC windows because pitting under neutralization creates a large effective discount; official race coverage repeatedly describes 'free pit stops' and pre-opened Safety Car windows.
rationale: F1 strategy groups explicitly watch for Safety Car/VSC windows because pitting under neutralization creates a large effective discount; official race coverage repeatedly describes 'free pit stops' and pre-opened Safety Car windows. Your table has no flag variable, but same-lap cross-driver anomalies can proxy neutralization events.
implementation_hint: Build race-lap features using only same-lap peer rows: share of pit stops on that lap, std/median of LapTime_Delta, count of extreme positive deltas, field size still circulating, and whether many drivers suddenly gain fresh-tyre pace together. Train a small specialist model on these race-lap aggregates and blend it with the base model, or gate the base score upward only when the neutralization proxy is high.
expected_effect: Should improve recall on abrupt multi-car stop waves and reduce missed late-race 'cheap stop' events that tyre-window heuristics cannot explain.
risk: Main trap is leakage if race-lap aggregates are computed using future-derived features or if CV splits let the same race appear across train/valid. Keep aggregates contemporaneous and split by race-year.
